title.en: CONTROL OR REGULATION OF ELECTRIC MOTORS, ELECTRIC GENERATORS OR DYNAMO-ELECTRIC CONVERTERS; CONTROLLING TRANSFORMERS, REACTORS OR CHOKE COILS

Arrangements for starting electric motors or dynamo-electric converters (starting of synchronous motors with electronic commutators H02P6/20, H02P6/22; starting dynamo-electric motors rotating step by step H02P8/04; vector control H02P21/00)
Details {of starting control}
for starting dynamo-electric motors or dynamo-electric converters

Arrangements for stopping or slowing electric motors, generators, or dynamo-electric converters (stopping of synchronous motors with electronic commutators H02P6/24; stopping dynamo-electric motors rotating step by step H02P8/24; vector control H02P21/00)
Details {of stopping control}
for stopping or slowing an individual dynamo-electric motor or dynamo-electric converter
Arrangements specially adapted for regulating or controlling the speed or torque of electric motors that can be connected to two or more different electric power supplies (vector control H02P21/00)
Arrangements specially adapted for regulating or controlling the speed or torque of two or more electric motors (H02P6/04, H02P8/40 take precedence)
for speed regulation of two or more dynamo-electric motors in relation to one another
controlling combinations of DC and AC dynamo-electric motors (H02P5/46 takes precedence)
controlling two or more DC dynamo-electric motors (H02P5/46, H02P5/60 take precedence)
controlling two or more AC dynamo-electric motors (H02P5/46, H02P5/60 take precedence)

Arrangements for controlling synchronous motors or other dynamo-electric motors using electronic commutation dependent on the rotor position; Electronic commutators therefor (vector control H02P21/00)
{Arrangements for controlling doubly fed motors}
{Controlling linear motors}
{wherein the position is detected using the ripple of the current caused by the commutation}
Arrangements for controlling or regulating the speed or torque of more than one motor (H02P6/10 takes precedence)
Arrangements for speed regulation of a single motor wherein the motor speed is measured and compared with a given physical value so as to adjust the motor speed
Arrangements for controlling the speed or torque of a single motor (H02P6/10, H02P6/28 take precedence)
Arrangements for controlling torque ripple, e.g. providing reduced torque ripple
Monitoring commutation; Providing indication of commutation failure
Electronic commutators
Arrangements for starting (H02P6/08 takes precedence)
Arrangements for stopping
Arrangements for controlling single phase motors
Arrangements for controlling current (H02P6/10 takes precedence)
Arrangements for controlling the direction of rotation (H02P6/22 takes precedence)
Arrangements for controlling wound field motors, e.g. motors with exciter coils
Modelling or simulation for control purposes
Arrangements for regulating or controlling the speed or torque of electric DC motors
{wherein the position is detected using the ripple of the current caused by the commutator}
the DC motors being of the linear type
for controlling the direction of rotation of DC motors
for regulating or controlling an individual DC dynamo-electric motor by varying field or armature current
Arrangements for controlling dynamo-electric motors rotating step by step
{of linear motors}
specially adapted for single-phase or bi-pole stepper motors, e.g. watch-motors, clock-motors
Arrangements for starting
Control or stabilisation of current
Arrangements for controlling speed or speed and torque (H02P8/12, H02P8/22 take precedence)
Control of step size; Intermediate stepping, e.g. microstepping
Arrangements for stopping (H02P8/32 takes precedence)
Reducing overshoot or oscillation, e.g. damping
Monitoring operation (H02P8/36 takes precedence)
Protection against faults, e.g. against overheating or step-out; Indicating faults
Special adaptations for controlling two or more stepping motors
characterised by non-stepper motors being operated step by step
Arrangements for controlling electric generators for the purpose of obtaining a desired output
{Means for protecting the generator by using control (control effected upon generator excitation circuit to reduce harmful effects of overloads or transients H02P9/10)}
{Control circuits for doubly fed generators}
{wherein the generator is controlled by the requirements of the prime mover}
{Circuit arrangements for detecting rotor position}
Details {of the control}
Control effected upon non-electric prime mover and dependent upon electric output value of the generator
Control effected upon clutch or other mechanical power transmission means and dependent upon electric output value of the generator
Control of generator circuit during starting or stopping of driving means, e.g. for initiating excitation
Control effected upon generator excitation circuit to reduce harmful effects of overloads or transients, e.g. sudden application of load, sudden removal of load, sudden change of load
by variation of field (H02P9/08, H02P9/10 take precedence)
by variation of reluctance of magnetic circuit of generator
to obtain desired frequency without varying speed of the generator
Control of frequency and voltage in predetermined relation, e.g. constant ratio
Control of asynchronous generator by variation of capacitor
Arrangements for obtaining a constant output value at varying speed of the generator, e.g. on vehicle (H02P9/04 - H02P9/46 take precedence)
Arrangements for controlling dynamo-electric converters
for controlling dynamo-electric converters having a DC output
for controlling dynamo-electric converters having an AC output
Arrangements for controlling transformers, reactors or choke coils, for the purpose of obtaining a desired output
by tap-changing; by rearranging interconnections of windings
by sliding current collector along winding
by moving core, coil winding, or shield, e.g. by induction regulator
by varying magnetic bias
Arrangements for controlling dynamo-electric brakes or clutches (vector control H02P21/00)
Conjoint control of brakes and clutches
Arrangements for controlling dynamo-electric gears (vector control H02P21/00)
Arrangements or methods for the control of electric machines by vector control, e.g. by control of field orientation
{Control strategies in general, e.g. linear type, e.g. P, PI, PID, using robust control}
{specially adapted for high speeds, e.g. above nominal speed}
specially adapted for optimising the efficiency at low load
specially adapted for very low speeds
specially adapted for damping motor oscillations, e.g. for reducing hunting
Rotor flux based control involving the use of rotor position or rotor speed sensors
Stator flux based control involving the use of rotor position or rotor speed sensors
Observer control, e.g. using Luenberger observers or Kalman filters
Estimation or adaptation of machine parameters, e.g. flux, current or voltage
Current control, e.g. using a current control loop
Vector control not involving the use of rotor position or rotor speed sensors
Arrangements for starting
Arrangements for braking or slowing; Four quadrant control
{Vector control arrangements or methods not otherwise provided for in H02P21/00- H02P21/36}
Arrangements or methods for the control of AC motors characterised by a control method other than vector control
{Control strategies in general, e.g. linear type, e.g. P, PI, PID, using robust control}
{Characterised by the use of a particular software algorithm}
{specially adapted for high speeds, e.g. above nominal speed}
specially adapted for optimising the efficiency at low load
specially adapted for very low speeds
specially adapted for damping motor oscillations, e.g. for reducing hunting
Controlling the motor in four quadrants
Controlling based on slip frequency, e.g. adding slip frequency and speed proportional frequency
Controlling by adding a DC current
Observer control, e.g. using Luenberger observers or Kalman filters
Estimation or adaptation of motor parameters, e.g. rotor time constant, flux, speed, current or voltage
Controlling the angular speed of one shaft (H02P23/18 takes precedence)
Controlling the angular speed together with angular position or phase
Controlling the acceleration or deceleration
Controlling the speed digitally using a reference oscillator, a speed proportional pulse rate feedback and a digital comparator
Controlling the direction, e.g. clockwise or counterclockwise
Power factor control [PFC]
Controlling the motor by varying the switching frequency of switches connected to a DC supply and the motor phases
Direct torque control [DTC] or field acceleration method [FAM]
Arrangements or methods for the control of AC motors characterised by the kind of AC motor or by structural details
characterised by the kind of motor
characterised by the circuit arrangement or by the kind of wiring

Arrangements or methods for the control of AC motors characterised by the kind of supply voltage (of two or more motors H02P5/00; of synchronous motors with electronic commutators H02P6/00; of DC motors H02P7/00; of stepping motors H02P8/00)
using supply voltage with constant frequency and variable amplitude
using variable-frequency supply voltage, e.g. inverter or converter supply voltage

Arrangements for regulating or controlling electric motors, appropriate for both AC and DC motors (arrangements for starting electric motors H02P1/00; arrangements for stopping or slowing electric motors H02P3/00; control of motors that can be connected to two or more different electric power supplies H02P4/00; regulating or controlling the speed or torque of two or more electric motors H02P5/00; vector control H02P21/00)
{Control of angular speed of one shaft without controlling the prime mover}
Providing protection against overload without automatic interruption of supply (protection against faults of stepper motors H02P8/36)
by means of a separate brake
for preventing overspeed or under speed
for controlling one motor used for different sequential operations
Regulating or controlling the amount of current drawn or delivered by the motor for controlling the mechanical load
Reduction of harmonics
Controlling or determining the temperature of the motor or of the drive (H02P29/02 takes precedence)
Arrangements for regulating or controlling electric motors not provided for in groups H02P1/00 - H02P5/00, H02P7/00 or H02P21/00 - H02P29/00
Special adaptation of control arrangements for generators
for water-driven turbines
for wind-driven turbines
for steam-driven turbines
for combustion engines
for aircraft
for ships
for railway vehicles
for motor vehicles, e.g. car alternators
Controlling arrangements characterised by the type of generator
of the asynchronous type
of the synchronous type
Indexing scheme relating to controlling arrangements characterised by the converter used
AC-AC converter stage controlled to provide a defined AC voltage
AC-DC converter stage controlled to provide a defined DC link voltage
Capacitive half bridge, i.e. resonant inverter having two capacitors and two switches
DC-DC step-up or step-down converter inserted between the power supply and the inverter supplying the motor, e.g. to control voltage source fluctuations, to vary the motor speed
Boost converter, i.e. DC-DC step up converter increasing the voltage between the supply and the inverter driving the motor
Buck converter, i.e. DC-DC step down converter decreasing the voltage between the supply and the inverter driving the motor
DC-link of current link type, e.g. typically for thyristor bridges, having an inductor in series with rectifier
Power factor correction [PFC] circuit generating the DC link voltage for motor driving inverter
Indexing scheme relating to controlling arrangements characterised by the means for detecting the position of the rotor
Motor rotor position determination based on the detected or calculated phase inductance, e.g. for a Switched Reluctance Motor
Determination of the rotor position, e.g. initial rotor position, during standstill or low speed operation
Determination of the rotor position by using two different methods and/or motor models

Motor variable determination based on the ON-resistance of a power switch, i.e. the voltage across the switch is measured during the ON state of the switch and used to determine the current in the motor and to calculate the speed
Motor speed determination based on the current and/or voltage without using a tachogenerator or a physical encoder
Determination or estimation of the rotor position or other motor parameters based on the analysis of high-frequency signals
Indexing scheme relating to controlling arrangements characterised by the control loops
Current loop, i.e. comparison of the motor current with a current reference
Power loop, i.e. comparison of the motor power with a power reference
Torque loop, i.e. comparison of the motor torque with a torque reference
Speed loop, i.e. comparison of the motor speed with a speed reference
Indexing scheme relating to controlling arrangements characterised by the type of motor
Asynchronous machines
Double rotor motors or generators, i.e. electromagnetic transmissions having double rotor with motor and generator functions, e.g. for electrical variable transmission
Synchronous machines, e.g. with permanent magnets or DC excitation
Doubly fed machines receiving two supplies both on the stator only wherein the power supply is fed to different sets of stator windings or to rotor and stator windings
Indexing scheme relating to controlling arrangements characterised by the waveform of the supplied voltage or current
Motors with neutral point connected to the power supply
Motors with neutral point disassociated, i.e. the windings ends are not connected directly to a common point
Polyphase motors supplied from a single-phase power supply or a DC power supply
Trapezoidal waveform
PWM with fixed limited number of pulses per period
Sinusoidal waveform
Different type of waveforms depending on the mode of operation
